career_angle: Pipecat is genuinely worth learning as an open-source voice agent framework; just don't lock yourself into AgentCore Runtime before comparing Fly.io, Modal, or self-hosted alternatives on latency and cost.

factors.rationale: AWS ML Blog carries weight for AWS products but this is vendor content marketing, not independent analysis
Describes architectural patterns and promises code samples but shows zero latency benchmarks, no cost comparisons, no before/after metrics
Part 1 of a tutorial series with deployment guidance and code -- should be reproducible if you have an AWS account
AWS and Pipecat co-authored a post promoting AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime -- textbook vendor content dressed as education
Voice agent challenges (latency, jitter, scaling) are real and well-documented; AgentCore Runtime itself is too new to have independent validation
Names specific transports (WebSocket, WebRTC, telephony) but no concrete numbers on latency, throughput, or cost versus alternatives
AWS cloud infra is battle-tested and Pipecat is a legit open-source project, but Bedrock AgentCore is brand new with no production track record
